Another Hrunting/Yggdrasil Mark I armor prototype:

Armor Information:

Design.

Derived from two separate projects, YGGDRASIL and HRUNTING, the prototype was initially intended as an early version of the MJOLNIR armor, which was designed for use by SPARTAN-IIs.[4] One of the first refinements of the UNSC's line of combat exoskeletons, the Mark I Defense Armor was a one-person war machine armed with a variety of devastating weapons.

The HRUNTING/YGGDRASIL suit's power supply came from a nuclear fusion reactor. Unlike the advanced MJOLNIR armor, the pilot did not require biological augmentation processes to use it and the exoskeleton did not recycle fluids. Although the HUD of both armors is similar, the HRUNTING/YGGDRASIL's weapons system and innovative aiming negated a cerebral link. For communications, the suit used a simple laser and radio for communications. The prototype was much more expensive to build than MJOLNIR, so it was not widely produced.

Equipment

The suit made use of a number of high-tech innovations to operate effectively in combat. One of these improvements was an integrated jetpack. This jetpack utilized a total of four stabilizers and four afterburning thrusters located at the front and rear of the suit. Directional control appeared to be provided by balance changes exerted by the operator. When not in use, the thrusters would slot into the suit to be protected from external damage. Another useful addition was the Bubble Shield reverse-engineered by ONI. The nature of the shield allowed it to be activated immediately when the suit received enemy fire. However, the suit lacked the constant, adjustable Energy Shields used by the MJOLNIR Armor.

Armament

The suit's main strength lay in the large number of weapon systems it possessed, each integrated directly into the exoskeleton's infrastructure. The right arm ended in the six-barrel, 850-round Lucifer AMGG, which had an integrated targeting system. A larger Grenade Launcher than the machine gun was mounted on the machine gun, accompanied by an ammunition
